original_image_tags: This is the reverse side of a document page. The paper is aged and yellowed. Visible at the top are two punched holes with reinforcement rings. The very faint text from the front of the page shows through the paper, with the words "UNITED STATES GOVERNMENT" and "Memorandum" partially discernible upside down. There are two pieces of yellowed tape, one at the top right and a smaller one on the right edge. The bottom of the page contains two stamped ink notations.

elements.description: Back of a yellowed document page with reversed text from the front side showing through the paper. Top edge has two punch holes. A small piece of brown tape is on the top right corner, and a darker square tape is on the middle right edge. Bottom section contains three visible blue ink stamps: one says 'RECEIVED APR 18 3 45 PM '50 INTERNAL SECURITY, SN FBI U.S. DEPT. OF JUSTICE'; another says 'RECEIVED APR 19 9:53 AM '50 RECORDED IN FILE CORRESPONDENCE F.B.I. U.S. DEPT. OF JUSTICE'; a third stamp contains the word 'APPROVED' with a signature.

judge.reason: Both descriptions agree on the main visual content (back of page, punch holes, tape, stamps) but Description B adds specific stamp text while Description A only notes their presence, creating no contradiction.
